sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Concussion follow-up screening in physio settings should identify patients recovering normally, patients needing graded activity support, and patients who need urgent medical review for delayed complications.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Headache, dizziness, light sensitivity, nausea, or fogginess after head injury
- Neck pain, balance difficulty, fatigue, irritability, or poor concentration
- Reduced exercise tolerance or symptom flare with cognitive effort
- Sleep disturbance and reduced confidence with work or sport return

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Escalate urgently for worsening headache, repeated vomiting, seizure, focal neurological deficit, anticoagulation concern, severe drowsiness, or major behavioural change
- Concussion often overlaps with neck pain, vestibular symptoms, and anxiety, but serious intracranial deterioration must stay front of mind early
- Return to risk activity should be staged and symptom-informed

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Screen red flags and current function rather than assuming all post-head-injury symptoms are benign
- Advise relative rest only for the immediate period, then gradual return to tolerated activity
- Workers must not return to safety-critical duties while symptomatic - this includes operating heavy plant or machinery, working at heights, entering confined spaces, or performing any task where impaired reaction time or cognitive function creates a risk of serious injury or death. Formal medical clearance is required before return to safety-critical roles
- Escalate if symptoms are worsening, persistent beyond expected recovery, or associated with major neurological concerns

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Assess exertional tolerance, balance, cervicogenic contribution, and symptom triggers
- Build a graded return to work, training, or daily activity plan
- Coordinate medical review, neurovestibular management, or multidisciplinary care if recovery stalls or symptom burden remains high

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Document the original mechanism, any loss of consciousness, red flags screened, and the return-to-activity advice provided
